title: FS: Real FUSE and overlay filesystem runtime
description: Implement the FUSE/overlay runtime described in specs/06-fuse-overlay-runtime.md so OSIx can mount snapshots as real branchable writable filesystems instead of only materialized restore copies.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime supports a shared MountRuntime API, materialized fallback, overlayfs mode, FUSE mode, watch integration, unmount/status/recover flows, security checks, and platform-aware tests.

title: FS-M0: Introduce mount runtime interface
description: Create the runtime boundary for mount implementations and move the current materialized mount behavior behind it.
acceptance_criteria: MountRuntime interface exists; materialized runtime implements it; mount metadata records selected mode; existing mount, diff, snapshot, and restore tests pass through the runtime interface.

title: Define MountRuntime API and mode types
description: Add MountRuntime, MountMode, UnmountOptions, Status, Diff, and Snapshot runtime APIs matching the filesystem runtime spec.
acceptance_criteria: Public/internal API has typed mount modes auto, overlay, fuse, materialized; callers can request mount, unmount, status, diff, and snapshot through one interface.

title: Move materialized mount behind MountRuntime
description: Refactor current mount behavior into a materialized runtime implementation rather than direct top-level restore-copy logic.
acceptance_criteria: `osix mount --mode materialized` uses the runtime implementation; existing materialized mount tests pass; no CLI behavior regression for current portable fallback.

title: Record selected mount mode in metadata
description: Extend mount metadata and status output to include mode, upper/work paths where relevant, process id where relevant, and lifecycle state.
acceptance_criteria: mount.json records selected mode; `osix mount` prints mode; status can show source digest, mode, branch, rw flag, target, and runtime paths.

title: Implement mount mode selection policy
description: Implement `--mode auto|overlay|fuse|materialized` and enforce explicit-mode failure when requested runtime prerequisites are unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: `--mode auto` selects overlay, then FUSE, then materialized according to platform availability; explicit overlay/fuse requests fail clearly if unavailable; tests cover selection behavior with mocked availability.

title: FS-M1: Linux overlayfs runtime
description: Implement privileged Linux overlayfs mounting with local lowerdirs, upperdir/workdir management, unmount/status/recover commands, and upperdir snapshot diffs.
acceptance_criteria: On Linux with privileges, `osix mount --mode overlay REF TARGET` creates a real overlay mount; mutations land in upperdir; snapshot emits only upperdir changes and whiteouts; unavailable platforms skip tests clearly.

title: Prepare overlay lowerdir chain
description: Extract or materialize parent snapshot chain into read-only lowerdir layers suitable for kernel overlayfs.
acceptance_criteria: Mount prep creates deterministic lowerdir directories; decrypted layers are protected with safe permissions; chain order matches restore order; corrupt/missing layers fail clearly.

title: Implement kernel overlay mount and unmount
description: Call platform mount/unmount operations for overlayfs with lowerdir, upperdir, workdir, and target management.
acceptance_criteria: `osix mount --mode overlay` mounts on capable Linux hosts; `osix unmount TARGET` unmounts cleanly; explicit overlay mode fails clearly without privileges or kernel support.

title: Snapshot overlay upperdir changes
description: Generate OSIx diff layers directly from overlay upperdir while translating overlay whiteouts into OCI whiteouts.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot after overlay mutations includes only upperdir changes; deletes become OCI whiteouts; restored snapshot matches mounted filesystem state.

title: Add overlayfs platform tests
description: Add integration tests for overlayfs that run only when Linux privileges and kernel support are available.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay tests skip with clear messages when prerequisites are missing; on capable Linux they verify mount, write, delete, snapshot, restore, and unmount.

title: FS-M2: Rootless FUSE runtime
description: Implement a FUSE runtime that presents parent snapshots plus writable upper state as a merged branchable filesystem.
acceptance_criteria: `osix mount --mode fuse REF TARGET` exposes a merged writable filesystem; snapshot after FUSE writes restores correctly; dirty tracking matches verification tree diff.

title: Select and integrate Go FUSE library
description: Resolve the open question between bazil.org/fuse, hanwen/go-fuse, or a platform adapter, then integrate the chosen dependency behind the runtime interface.
acceptance_criteria: Decision record explains library choice; build tags/platform handling are clear; dependency compiles on supported platforms.

title: Implement FUSE merged tree reads
description: Serve lookup, getattr, readdir, open, readlink, and read operations from merged parent layers plus upper state.
acceptance_criteria: Mounted FUSE tree shows expected files/directories/symlinks from source snapshot; read operations match restored materialized state; unsupported metadata returns clear errors.

title: Implement FUSE copy-on-write mutations
description: Support create, write, truncate, unlink, rename, mkdir, rmdir, chmod, chown where supported, and symlink behavior by writing to upper state and whiteout records.
acceptance_criteria: First write to lower file copies it to upper; creates and deletes are reflected in merged tree; rename and chmod behavior is tested; whiteouts survive remount.

title: Implement FUSE dirty tracking
description: Maintain dirty.json with dirty bytes, path operation states, and updatedAt timestamps for FUSE mounts.
acceptance_criteria: Dirty index updates on create/modify/delete/rename; dirty byte count is accurate enough for watch policy; verification diff can detect and repair stale/corrupt dirty index.

title: Snapshot FUSE upper state
description: Generate OSIx diff layers from the FUSE dirty index and upper files, with fallback to tree comparison when dirty tracking is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot after FUSE writes restores correctly; dirty-index and fallback paths produce equivalent final state; deterministic tar ordering is preserved.

title: Add FUSE platform tests
description: Add FUSE integration tests that run when Linux /dev/fuse or macFUSE is available and skip clearly otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify mount, reads, writes, deletes, renames, dirty tracking, snapshot, restore, and unmount on capable platforms; skip messages identify missing prerequisites.

title: FS-M3: Watch integration with filesystem runtime
description: Make watch consume runtime dirty bytes and flush runtime state before turn-boundary snapshots.
acceptance_criteria: `osix watch TARGET --max-dirty 512MiB` uses runtime dirty tracking when healthy; turn-boundary snapshots flush the runtime first; fallback scanning remains correct.

title: Read runtime dirty bytes in watch
description: Update watch scheduling to consume mount runtime dirty indexes before falling back to tree scans.
acceptance_criteria: Watch uses dirty.json when valid; corrupt or missing dirty index triggers verification scan; tests cover both paths.

title: Flush runtime before turn-boundary snapshots
description: Ensure watch calls runtime flush/quiesce before snapshotting when `--on-turn-boundary` is set.
acceptance_criteria: Turn-boundary watch snapshots include all writes flushed before boundary; active write failure is surfaced clearly; tests cover materialized and mocked runtime behavior.

title: FS-M4: Recovery and hardening
description: Add stale mount detection, recovery commands, cache permission checks, and platform prerequisite checks for the filesystem runtime.
acceptance_criteria: Simulated crash leaves upperdir recoverable; `osix mount recover TARGET` preserves dirty files; unsafe cache permissions are refused; platform prerequisite failures are clear.

title: Implement mount status and stale mount detection
description: Implement `osix mount status TARGET` with lifecycle state, pid checks, selected mode, upper/work paths, and stale mount detection.
acceptance_criteria: Status reports mounted/unmounted/stale states; stale pids are detected; status output is stable enough for scripting.

title: Implement mount recovery command
description: Implement `osix mount recover TARGET` to preserve upperdir data and repair or recreate runtime metadata after crashes or stale mounts.
acceptance_criteria: Simulated crash leaves upperdir recoverable; recover command preserves dirty files; corrupt dirty index can be rebuilt by scanning.

title: Enforce runtime cache and upperdir permissions
description: Refuse unsafe world-writable cache, upper, work, or decrypted layer directories and ensure new runtime directories use safe permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime-created sensitive directories use 0700 where appropriate; world-writable paths are refused; tests cover permission validation.

title: Add platform prerequisite checks
description: Detect overlayfs, FUSE, macFUSE, /dev/fuse, and mount privilege prerequisites before attempting explicit runtime modes.
acceptance_criteria: Explicit overlay/fuse requests fail before partial setup when prerequisites are missing; errors explain missing component; tests cover mocked prerequisite matrix.

title: Document FUSE and overlay runtime operations
description: Add user and operator docs for runtime modes, platform setup, unmount/recover, security expectations, and troubleshooting.
acceptance_criteria: Docs cover Linux overlayfs, Linux FUSE, macOS FUSE, auto mode selection, failure recovery, and known limitations.
